translations.descricao: Cellulitis is an acute bacterial infection of the dermis and subcutaneous tissue, usually caused by Streptococcus or Staphylococcus. It is characterized by erythema, warmth, edema, and local pain. It is one of the most common skin infections, with potential for complications such as abscesses or sepsis. In the Brazilian context, surveillance is essential in vulnerable populations.
translations.sus.indicacao: Surveillance and early detection of skin and soft tissue infections in primary care, especially in cases of exposure to risk factors such as wounds or comorbidities.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Vulnerable populations, including diabetics, immunocompromised individuals, elderly people, and individuals in endemic areas for related zoonoses.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Diagnosis and management of cellulitis in patients with signs of acute skin infection, prioritizing empirical treatment.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The prevalence of cellulitis is estimated at 1-5% in general populations, with higher incidence in diabetics (up to 20%). In Brazil, it accounts for about 10% of skin infections treated in health services.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global incidence of 24.6 cases per 1,000 person-years in adults. In Brazil, it is estimated at 15-20 cases per 1,000 inhabitants annually in urban areas.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Low mortality (1-5%) in uncomplicated cases, but up to 20% in associated sepsis. In Brazil, it contributes to 2-3% of deaths from systemic infections.
